topic: Someone receives a friend request from a childhood bully
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification popped up on my screen, a stark, unwelcome intrusion into my otherwise calm afternoon. My stomach immediately tightened, a cold knot forming right beneath my ribs. It was a request. From Mark. Mark Peterson. The name alone sent a jolt of sheer dread through me. My fingers hovered over the screen, trembling slightly as I considered whether to accept or block. Every instinct screamed at me to delete the message, to erase this digital tether to a painful past. I stared at the profile picture—a slightly too-confident smirk I remembered vividly from middle school. A wave of heat flushed my neck, and I quickly minimized the app, focusing intently on the dust motes dancing in the sunlight filtering through the window.

[story 2]

I almost dropped my phone. The vibration against the wooden desk felt like an electric shock. When I finally looked down, the name flashed: 'Mark P.' My breath hitched, shallow and fast. For a solid minute, I couldn't move, my gaze locked onto the screen as if it held a dangerous secret. My palms began to sweat, slicking the phone casing. *No. Absolutely not.* I squeezed my eyes shut, trying to force a logical response, but the image of Mark’s sneering face flashed behind my eyelids. When I finally forced my eyes open, I snatched the device and navigated straight to the settings menu, my movements jerky and overly deliberate as I searched for the undo button.

[story 3]

A strange, hollow feeling settled over me, heavy and suffocating. I reread the request three times, each repetition digging a deeper furrow in my brow. It was impossible. Mark had been the architect of so much of my awkward adolescence. Now, this phantom connection reappeared, demanding attention. I leaned back in my chair, the cheap plastic groaning under my weight, and let out a slow, shaky exhale. My jaw clenched so tightly I could feel the strain in my temples. I slowly scrolled through my contacts, my heart thumping a frantic rhythm against my ribs, before finally tapping the reject button with a decisive, almost violent motion.
